content:
📜 2. Betrayal Trials – Balancing Morality & Consequence

📜 Refining how betrayal is handled when a player is caught.

✔ New Betrayal Trial System (Based on Detection Rolls & Group Judgment)
• A betrayal does not automatically result in a trial.
• If a player commits an act of betrayal, they must pass a stealth roll (modified by alignment).
• If the roll fails, the group can hold a trial.

✔ How Moral Alignment Affects Betrayal Success:
• Players who lean into negative alignments (Opportunist, Betrayer) get a bonus to stealth.
• Players who align positively (Loyalist, Martyr) struggle to betray without being caught.
• If a player is too “good,” their betrayal requires a much higher roll to succeed.

✔ The Trial Process:
• The betrayed faction/class votes on the fate of the betrayer.
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 478 (chunk 478/555)
